summary: Inventive China Cat → Eleven segue & soaring Saint Stephen‑>Feedback
blurb: The set bursts with a wild China Cat Sunflower → The Eleven transition and a soaring He Was A Friend Of Mine. Jerry’s guitar is enthusiastic throughout, while the Rhythm Devils deliver a powerful Saint Stephen > Feedback climax. Overall it ranks as one of the more adventurous 1969 performances.
review: On May 24‑1979 at the Seminole Indian Reservation the Dead took listeners on a daring trip through some of their most exploratory material. The night opens with Turn On Your Lovelight and quickly dives into a seamless China Cat Sunflower → The Eleven segue that fans still cite as a highlight of this era, followed by a heartfelt He Was A Friend Of Mine. The second half builds into an all‑out Rhythm Devils climax—Alligator into Drums and a feedback‑laden Saint Stephen > Feedback that feels like a volcanic release. Jerry’s “great gar” enthusiasm shines throughout, though the drum work is a mixed bag: one source praises its power while another calls it “shoes in the dryer.” The pristine KPFA soundboard capture (gd69-05-24.sbd.kpfa.16177.sbeok.shnf) presents these moments with crystal‑clear balance, making this the definitive listening version despite a brief drum pause and a streaming glitch during The Eleven.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: One recording is strongly positive, praising the adventurous segues and Jerry’s playing; the second is mixed but only notes a few weak spots (drums, Doin' That Rag). The overall consensus leans toward a solidly good show with notable high points, warranting a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Pristine soundboard capture with crisp mix and minimal technical flaws, showcasing the band's adventurous improvisations clearly.

key_highlights: China Cat Sunflower → The Eleven segue showcases daring improvisation.
Saint Stephen > Feedback delivers a feedback‑laden climax that caps the show’s intensity.
He Was A Friend Of Mine provides soaring vocal harmonies and lyrical depth.
The KPFA soundboard source is crisp and balanced, despite a brief drum pause.
song_highlights: He Was A Friend Of Mine

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s playing is described as enthusiastic and lyrical, delivering a ‘great gar’ vibe that fuels the adventurous jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey contributes to a powerful Rhythm Devils conclusion, though one source notes a brief pause before Saint Stephen.
band_performance.Billy: Bill’s drumming is highlighted as strong in the climax but also critiqued for lacking cohesion on other parts of the set.

must_listen_sequences: He Was A Friend Of Mine
And We Bid You Good Night
